prompt: Why is this database query slow when the execution plan looks optimal? The table has 100 million rows. (variant 0)
We have inconsistent error handling across three services. Should we standardize on a wrapper or let each handle it? (variant 1)
Implement a graceful degradation strategy when a critical dependency is unavailable but non-blocking. (variant 2)
The WebSocket connection drops intermittently under load. Debug the heartbeat and backpressure handling. (variant 3)
Should we cache query results or denormalize the data? Trade-offs for a 10GB dataset. (variant 4)
This function signature changed but callers are still using the old one. How should we handle the migration? (variant 5)
Implement a circuit breaker for external API calls that fail intermittently. (variant 6)
Design a request deduplication system for idempotent operations across retries. (variant 7)
The async batch processor works locally but hangs in production. Diagnose the scheduling issue. (variant 8)
We need to route different user types to different backends. What is the cleanest way to inject this logic? (variant 9)
